- Bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering or equivalent
- Experience in audits related to business processes, risk mitigation, program compliance, or internal audits

jobs.description:
Prime Air is seeking an experienced engineer to monitor hardware quality and compliance issues in our drone delivery operations. Are you interested in solving problems for a quickly scaling business using state-of-the-art technology?

Key job responsibilities
In this role you will:
- Monitor quality issues in the service fleet of delivery drones and our supporting systems
- Triage and resolve quality issues by performing a Root Cause and Corrective Action (RCCA) analysis
- Drive the resolution of in-service issues by coordinating the actions of other Prime Air engineering and operations teams
- Identify compliance issues (compliance to regulation or internal procedures) and solve the issues or pass them to compliance stakeholders
- Work with compliance stakeholders to maintain a comprehensive risk-based audit program

A day in the life
0800-0830: Brief status of drone fleet and discuss prominent issues.
0830-1000: Investigate a quality issue found by field maintenance technicians. Work cross-functionally with Supplier and Assembly Quality Teams to see where the issue originated. Be sure to consider all possible failure points across manufacturing and operations.
1000-1200: Investigate variances in crew logs. Meet with drone operators, regulatory SMEs, and the Learning team to make standards clearer for crews.
1200-1230: Lunch with a view of Mt. Rainier.
1230-1700: Focus time designing a process for part scrapping that scales to over 100 sites.

About the team
The Quality Engineering team at Prime Air ensures the quality of parts coming from suppliers, assembly processes, and in-service drones. The In-Service Quality (ISQ) team covers the last phase of the product quality lifecycle. We monitor for quality issues and process breaks where drones are actually used to deliver to for our customers. Our scope is broad because problems at this phase can come from a part design, software issues, user documentation, or external factors. Anywhere we see a risk, we respond, coordinating with other teams and resources across the company to improve safety, efficiency, or customer experience.

jobs.title: In-Service/Fleet/Field Quality Engineer, Quality Engineering Team

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 2+ years of employee and performance management experience
- Bachelor's degree or equivalent, or 2+ years of Amazon (blue badge/FTE) experience
- Work a flexible schedule/shift/work area, including weekends, nights, and/or holidays
jobs.description:
This position requires in-role training at an operating site which will be 2+ weeks in duration. This training will be located 50+ miles away from the home site location. All travel accommodations for training will be booked and paid for by Amazon.

Please note: this role requires open flexibility to work 10 hour overnight shifts (late afternoon to early morning hours) and will be scheduled on a front-half (Sunday – Wednesday) or back-half (Wednesday – Saturday) schedule. Shifts will be assigned upon starting in the role and could be subject to change based upon business need.

ABOUT AMAZON

Our Worldwide Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Area Manager. In this role, you will lead a team of hourly employees and will be responsible for engaging your team during their shifts to maintain the highest levels of safety, quality, attendance, and performance. You will also play a key part in maintaining our customer expectations to ensure customer orders are delivered at the right time, to the right location.

Our Operation’s workflow has three major components: First mile - where the product is housed and ready for order; Middle mile - where the order is hauled to your area; and Last mile - when the product is delivered to the customer’s door. Please note that all workflows have slight building variations, but one thing is constant: our vision and dedication to the customer.

Rapid and Rural Logistics (R2L) is an exciting organization within Amazon specializing in delighting our customers with speed in which we can make items appear on their doorsteps. Rural Super Rural (RSR) is a subset of R2L dedicated to ensuring our customers receive their deliveries and are satisfied with their service, even in our most remote area. We are a combination of operations and support teams aligned to continually strive to innovate the last mile delivery process while bringing smiles to our customer’s faces. Come build the future with us!

Key job responsibilities
- Support, mentor, and motivate your team
- Manage safety, quality, productivity, and customer delivery promises
- Collaborate with all support teams including Safety, Ops Integration, Loss Prevention and Human Resources to develop plans to meet business objectives
- Lift up to 49 pounds, stand and walk during shifts lasting up to 12 hours, and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach, as well as continuously climb and descend stairs
- Work in an environment where the noise level varies and is subject to variable temperatures and weather (delivery stations include outside loading departments, and grocery locations include refrigerated environments)
- Work a flexible schedule (weekends and/or overnight shifts)

jobs.basic_qualifications:
BASIC QUALIFICATIONS
- A completed Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited university
- 7+ years of Direct management experience, including a salaried workforce, in a manufacturing, production, distribution, or logistics environment
- Experience with performance metrics, process improvement, and Lean methodologies
- Experience with the full staffing lifecycle, including interviewing, hiring, performance management, promotions and terminations
- Candidates must be flexible to work a variety of hours as business demands, including overnight, weekends and holidays
jobs.description:
Our Senior Operations Managers play a crucial role in exceeding expectations and providing a superb customer experience; critical to Amazon’s success which is built on a foundation of customer obsession, and innovation. This position is a multi-faceted role requiring the ability to balance strategy and execution. The Senior Operations Manager is responsible for all aspects of the operations including, people management and development, process, and meeting or exceeding the expectations and promise made to our customers. You will lead a dynamic team of Operations and Area Managers to achieve operational excellence through coaching and mentoring the team; driving employee engagement, and building leadership bench strength.

As a senior leader you will oversee salaried and hourly employees, in a fast-paced, complex operations facility up to a million square feet.

Our Senior Operations Managers serve as the face of the organization to potentially thousands of employees and the community where the center is located. This position offers unlimited career potential as we continue to grow our global footprint.


Strategic Responsibilities & Deliverables:
- Owns and delivers the operational budget for functional area, including safety, productivity, financial and labor planning, and operational goals for outbound, or inbound operations
- Responsible for providing strategic level/long-term planning (3, 6, and 12 months out) including labor planning, rate forecasting, and peak season planning
- Drives continuous improvement projects to optimize operations and improve productivity to meet and exceed business objectives; works on strategic projects that have total building and network-wide impact. Effectively leverages the Operations and Area Managers to solicit ideas and understand problems and challenges in the building.
- Networks with other leaders across the network to solicit and share process improvements and drive efficiencies and cost savings.
- Establishes objectives and metrics for safety, quality, productivity, and customer experience
- Sets clear goals and expectations for Operations and Area Managers. Measures performance, provides feedback, and holds leaders accountable
- Mentors and develops leaders and staff
- Participates in recruiting to identify and evaluate associates and internal/external candidates for promotion and hire.
- Works closely with support staff (HR, Finance, Maintenance Engineering, Safety, IT and other leaders) to build and secure support and resources for projects and initiatives



Additional Job Elements:
- Lift and move totes up to 49 pounds each
- Regular bending, lifting, stretching and reaching both below the waist and above the head
- Must be able to stand/walk for up to 10-12 hours
- Should be able to work in environments with variable noise levels, lighting conditions and temperature variation.
- Able to access all areas of building (ascending and descending ladders, stairs, gangways safely and without limitation

jobs.title: Sr. Operations Manager

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of non-internship professional software development experience
- 2+ years of non-internship design or architecture (design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and existing systems experience
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or equivalent work experience
- Experience debugging, diagnosing, and troubleshooting complex, production software issues
- Experience developing and maintaining Linux distributions with build frameworks for embedded or edge computing environments, including system administration, driver management, and system configuration.
- Experience with ARM architectures (ARM64/AArch64, ARMv7, or other ARM variants) including cross-compilation toolchains and platform-specific optimizations.
- Knowledge of update systems, partition management (A/B updates), system recovery mechanisms, rollback strategies, and bootloader configuration (U-Boot, GRUB, etc.).
- Experience integrating device drivers and kernel modules into Linux build systems.
- Knowledge using Rust/C/C++ for system-level programming and Python for automation and tooling.
- 3+ years deploying and managing custom Linux solutions across diverse hardware configurations at fleet scale, including validation of BSP functionality, interfaces, and peripherals.
- Experience with CI/CD pipelines, version control systems (Git), and automated testing frameworks.
jobs.description:
We're seeking a Software Development Engineer with deep expertise in Linux distribution development and Board Support Package (BSP) engineering for ARM-based compute platforms. This role is critical to Amazon Robotics operations, where you'll build and maintain custom Linux solutions that power compute devices across our global fulfillment and robotics network. You'll be responsible for developing scalable BSP solutions and managing large-scale deployment operations across diverse hardware ecosystems supporting Amazon's robotics infrastructure.

Work Environment

This role supports Amazon Robotics operations and may require occasional travel to fulfillment centers and robotics sites for hardware validation, troubleshooting, or deployment support. The position involves working with distributed teams across multiple time zones and participating in on-call rotations to ensure 24/7 support for critical robotics and fulfillment operations.

Why Amazon Robotics?

Join a team that's building the technology infrastructure powering Amazon's global robotics and fulfillment network. Your work will directly impact millions of customer orders and enable Amazon's commitment to fast, reliable delivery. You'll work with pioneering robotics technology at an unprecedented scale, solving complex technical challenges that few organizations face. We offer opportunities for growth, learning, and making a

meaningful impact on one of the world's most sophisticated robotics and logistics operations.

Key job responsibilities
- Design, develop, and maintain Linux BSP images for ARM compute platforms deployed across Amazon's robotics and fulfillment network using Yocto Project or custom build systems

- Create and maintain Yocto recipes, build configurations, package recipes, and system layers optimized for ARM architectures and robotics/fulfillment center requirements

- Implement A/B update mechanisms with dual-boot partition systems and rollback capabilities to ensure zero-downtime updates across the fleet

- Integrate device drivers and kernel modules specific to robotics and fulfillment center hardware requirements into Linux build systems

- Deploy BSP builds at scale across Amazon's global fulfillment and robotics network, managing deployments across hundreds of buildings and hundreds of thousands of devices

- Develop deployment automation for fleet management operations and validate system stability across diverse hardware configurations

- Perform validation of BSP functionality including hardware interfaces, peripherals, and system components

- Monitor and troubleshoot BSP deployments in production environments, responding to issues affecting robotics and fulfillment operations

- Develop automated testing frameworks for BSP validation across different hardware variants

- Ensure system security hardening and compliance with Amazon's security standards for robotics and edge compute devices

- Collaborate with robotics and fulfillment center operations teams to ensure minimal disruption during updates and maintenance windows

- Create and maintain documentation for build processes, deployment procedures, and troubleshooting guides

- Participate in on-call rotations to support critical robotics and fulfillment center operation

Professional Skills:
- Strong problem-solving abilities and analytical thinking
- Excellent communication skills for cross-functional collaboration

- Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

- Customer-obsessed mindset with focus on operational excellence

- Willingness to participate in on-call rotations to support critical operations

A day in the life
Your day typically starts by building Yocto recipes for new hardware platforms, ensuring the BSP stack is ready to support upcoming device variants. From there, you'll review overnight CI/CD build results, troubleshoot failed Yocto builds, and write BitBake recipes to integrate device drivers. You'll spend time on kernel configuration work to optimize boot time and memory usage, then sync with robotics workcell engineers to work through active issues — whether that's understanding new hardware requirements coming down the pipeline, debugging integration problems between the BSP and workcell control systems, or aligning on interface expectations for upcoming device variants. You'll investigate A/B update issues from test deployments and collaborate with operations teams on deployment plans for security patches across multiple fulfillment centers and robotics networks. You'll monitor fleet health dashboards, automate deployment tasks using Python, and work on improving the orchestration system based on real-world edge cases.

The rest of your day involves design reviews for new update mechanism features, code reviews for build system changes, mentoring team members on Yocto configurations, and updating deployment documentation. Later in the afternoon, you'll join the team standup to discuss upcoming BSP releases and deployment schedules. Throughout the day, you'll balance deep technical Linux systems with operational responsibilities, responding to deployment questions, monitoring fleet metrics, and staying ready to address critical issues affecting Amazon Robotics operations.

Amazon offers a full range of benefits that support you and eligible family members, including domestic partners and their children. Benefits can vary by location, the number of regularly scheduled hours you work, length of employment, and job status such as seasonal or temporary employment.

The benefits that generally apply to regular, full-time employees include:
- Medical, Dental, and Vision Coverage
- Maternity and Parental Leave Options
- Paid Time Off (PTO)
- 401(k) Plan  

If you are not sure that every qualification on the list above describes you exactly, we'd still love to hear from you!

At Amazon, we value people with unique backgrounds, experiences, and skillsets. If you’re passionate about this role and want to make an impact on a global scale, please apply!

About the team
Join a team at the forefront of Amazon Robotics technology infrastructure, managing one of the largest device fleets in Amazon Operations. Our team specializes in OS development, device lifecycle management, and custom Linux distributions for edge compute devices and robots, supporting operations across hundreds of fulfillment buildings worldwide. We maintain critical technology ecosystems that enable Amazon's fulfillment and robotics operations to run efficiently and reliably on a massive scale.

jobs.title: Software Development Engineer II, Robotics ARM Compute BSP

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Associate's degree in Telecommunications or related discipline, or equivalent work experience
- 2+ years of experience Inside Plant cabling installation in data center environments
- Knowledge of basic quality tools (e.g., defect categorization, root cause analysis, or quality reporting systems)
- Knowledge of fiber optic, copper (CAT 5e/6/6A) cabling installation standards
jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS) designs, plans, delivers, and operates all AWS global infrastructure. We keep the cloud running by supporting AWS data centers and the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that give customers continual access to innovation. We solve complex problems with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain.

You'll join software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other essential roles. You'll collaborate across AWS to deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing capacity at the lowest cost for customers.

In this role, you will:

Work with Network Deployment and Vendor Management teams to integrate quality assurance into cable installation processes throughout the project lifecycle. You'll participate in reviews of training curriculums and cabling design documents, providing Quality Assurance representation to ensure standards are met during cable installation activities. You'll influence partner teams and cabling vendors to adopt quality assurance checklists that validate conformance to both Amazon and jurisdictional standards during critical milestone checks for cable installation and testing activities.

You'll bring the voice of the customer forward by gathering feedback from internal stakeholders and cabling vendors, then refining quality processes based on their needs. You'll verify that cable installations meet standards by reviewing documentation and data from milestone inspections and cable validation testing. You'll drive cable validation testing adoption and defect management while establishing performance benchmarks to improve vendor performance and enhance quality metrics. You'll document and align cable installation standards and standard operating procedures as new technology and cabling methods integrate into our infrastructure. You’ll also conduct training with partner teams and provide guidelines to cabling vendors to ensure consistent execution across all network installations.

This role matters because cable installation quality directly impacts data center network reliability and customer experience. You'll own the quality process from participating in cabling design reviews through training delivery, ensuring our infrastructure meets the standards our customers depend on.

Key job responsibilities
- Support Quality Assurance related programs to drive world-class quality in network cabling in the new constructions of Data Centers to achieve the best operating, lowest cost infrastructure in the world. The Network Cabling Quality Technician will be obsessed with quality, engineering and design continuous improvements

- Partner with Network Deployment teams to adopt defined quality assurance checklists, gather feedback, and generate recommendations to improve checklists. Work with partner teams to track cabling scope changes and perform impact analysis to address business needs

- Partner with the Vendor Management team to drive cable vendor quality, provide vendor performance metrics through business reviews, and refine processes to improve vendor performance

- Support cable installation benchmark according to Amazon policies and industry standards; maintain cable installation standards, checklists, standard operating procedures and best practices guidelines

- Conduct Network Cabling Installation Quality Training Programs, and the creation of training materials, to support internal and external partner and stakeholder teams

- Collaborate with stakeholder teams to capture metrics data such as inspection reports and cable test results; track defects for root cause analysis, generate recommendations, evaluate vendor performances and escalate issues to respective owners for remediation plans

- Track and report on project milestones, vendor performance metrics, and quality KPIs including First Pass Yield, defect rates, and schedule adherence across multiple concurrent projects

- Support continuous improvement initiatives by capturing lessons learned, facilitating post-project reviews, and implementing process improvements to enhance future project delivery

- Identify opportunities to reduce Cost of Poor Quality and improve overall project efficiency

- Regional and Local Travel up to 25% to attend site visits and meetings for onsite quality standards review and collect feedback from onsite personnel

A day in the life
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a highly innovative company that collaborates across disciplines to produce new technology that changes our world. The AWS Quality Team is a fast-growing global team that is rethinking the implementation and design for the critical arteries that connect the various pieces of hardware inside AWS’ data centers and edge sites.

As a member of this team, you will be charged with visiting our data centers and ensuring that we are following AWS and Industry standards while also collecting lessons learned so we are continually improving. You will work directly with engineers, managers, and operations teams across the company to develop and improve network pathway and installation standards based on these observations and lessons learned.

jobs.title: Network Cabling Quality Technician, GND QA

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Associate's degree in Telecommunications or related discipline, or equivalent work experience
- 2+ years of experience Inside Plant cabling installation in data center environments
- Experience managing quality across the full project lifecycle, from design review through installation validation and defect remediation.
- Knowledge of small to moderate scale program coordination with internal team and contractors
jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS) designs, plans, delivers, and operates all AWS global infrastructure. We keep the cloud running by supporting AWS data centers and the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that give customers continual access to innovation. We solve complex problems with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain.

You'll join software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other essential roles. You'll collaborate across AWS to deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing capacity at the lowest cost for customers.

In this role, you will:

Work with Network Deployment and Vendor Management teams to integrate quality assurance into cable installation processes throughout the project lifecycle. You'll participate in reviews of training curriculums and cabling design documents, providing Quality Assurance representation to ensure standards are met during cable installation activities. You'll influence partner teams and cabling vendors to adopt quality assurance checklists that validate conformance to both Amazon and jurisdictional standards during critical milestone checks for cable installation and testing activities.

You'll bring the voice of the customer forward by gathering feedback from internal stakeholders and cabling vendors, then refining quality processes based on their needs. You'll verify that cable installations meet standards by reviewing documentation and data from milestone inspections and cable validation testing. You'll drive cable validation testing adoption and defect management while establishing performance benchmarks to improve vendor performance and enhance quality metrics. You'll document and align cable installation standards and standard operating procedures as new technology and cabling methods integrate into our infrastructure. You’ll also organize training with partner teams and provide guidelines to cabling vendors to ensure consistent execution across all network installations.

This role matters because cable installation quality directly impacts data center network reliability and customer experience. You'll own the quality process from participating in cabling design reviews through training delivery, ensuring our infrastructure meets the standards our customers depend on.

This is an opportunity to leverage and grow your existing program management, quality assurance and engineering experience in an innovative and highly collaborative environment where the results of your work will directly impact AWS and its customers.

Key job responsibilities
- Support Quality Assurance related programs to drive world-class quality in network cabling deployments in new and existing Data Centers to achieve the best operating, lowest cost infrastructure in the world. The Cabling Quality Program Manager will be obsessed with driving quality structured cabling solutions.

- Partner with Network Deployment teams to adopt defined quality assurance checklists, gather feedback, and generate recommendations to improve checklists. Work with partner teams to track cabling scope changes and perform impact analysis to address business needs

- Partner with the Vendor Management team to drive cable vendor quality, provide vendor performance metrics through business reviews, and refine processes to improve vendor performance

- Support cable installation benchmarks according to Amazon policies and industry standards; maintain cable installation standards, checklists, standard operating procedures and best practices guidelines

- Organize Network Cabling Installation Quality Training Programs, and the creation of training materials, to support internal and external partner and stakeholder teams

- Collaborate with stakeholder teams to capture metrics data such as inspection reports and cable test results; track defects for root cause analysis, generate recommendations, evaluate vendor performances and escalate issues to respective owners for remediation plans

- Track and report on project milestones, vendor performance metrics, and quality KPIs including First Pass Yield, defect rates, and schedule adherence across current and future network cabling projects

- Support continuous improvement initiatives by capturing lessons learned, facilitating post-project reviews, and implementing process improvements to enhance future project delivery

- Identify opportunities to reduce Cost of Poor Quality and improve overall project efficiency

- Regional and Local Travel up to 25% to attend site visits and meetings for onsite quality standards review and collect feedback from onsite personnel

A day in the life
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a highly innovative company that collaborates across disciplines to produce new technology that changes our world. The AWS Quality Team is a fast-growing global team that is rethinking the implementation and design for the critical arteries that connect the various pieces of hardware inside AWS’ data centers and edge sites.

As a member of this team, you will be charged with visiting our data centers and ensuring that we are following AWS and Industry standards while also collecting lessons learned so we are continually improving. You will work directly with engineers, managers, and operations teams across the company to develop and improve network pathway and installation standards based on these observations and lessons learned.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, Manufacturing Engineering, or an equivalent field
- 7+ years of experience developing test infrastructure to meet the needs of testing space craft or similarly complex systems.
- 5+ years of experience in design and validation of hardware systems, including use of CAD (Siemens NX or similar)
- 2+ years of experience with automating hardware testing and data analysis using Python, LabVIEW, C++ or similar
jobs.description:
Amazon Leo is Amazon's low Earth orbit satellite network. Our mission is to deliver fast, reliable internet connectivity to customers beyond the reach of existing networks. From individual households to schools, hospitals, businesses, and government agencies, Amazon Leo will serve people and organizations operating in locations without reliable connectivity.

Export Control Requirement: Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

We are looking for an experienced Sr. Test Development Engineer to design and implement test infrastructure for mechanical satellite systems that support the development and testing of satellite flight hardware, holding the gate to flight readiness. This is a fast-paced, intellectually challenging opportunity to work with thought leaders in multiple technology areas. You’ll have relentlessly high standards for yourself and everyone you work with, and you’ll be constantly looking for ways to improve your product’s performance, quality, and reliability.

Key job responsibilities
• Design, analyze, develop, and implement test equipment and infrastructure used in satellite flight hardware development and qualification.
• Author, refine and implement validation, verification, acceptance and qualification test procedures.
• Develop mechatronics focused test automation and data acquisition.
• Develop post-processing tools to decompose test data into desired metrics.
• Lead and participate in design reviews, and risk assessments, to contribute to the continuous improvement of satellite hardware performance and reliability.
• Work concurrently with the design engineers to provide design for testability (DFT) feedback throughout the design process
• Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, including design, manufacturing, systems, reliability, and quality engineers, to gather information, share findings, and develop solutions.


A day in the life
As a Amazon Leo Sr. Test Development Engineer, you will own the design and implementation of automated test equipment used to validate satellite hardware designs from initial prototypes, though functional testing, and into pilot production. You will work with design and manufacturing teams to specify test equipment requirements, develop test plans, and execute test campaigns to support development and production test initiatives. The ideal candidate is self-motivated and comfortable operating in a fast-paced development environment.

About the team
This team is organized within the satellite mechanical design team to accelerate the design iteration process and ensure on-time delivery of the most stable and successful product possible. The tasks and daily focus change rapidly, and you will often take on new and exciting responsibilities as required to ensure project success. Expect to be deployed to complex problems and novel technical challenges that push you to develop new skills. You’ll be constantly looking for ways to improve your product’s performance, quality and cost.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Telecommunications, Mechanical, or Structural Engineering, or related discipline, or equivalent work experience.
- 4+ years of experience in network pathway infrastructure design or installation in data center environments
- Experience performing failure analysis and recommending engineering-level corrective actions to prevent recurrence
- Proficiency of NEMA VE1/VE2 or IEC 61537 for cable tray construction and installation.
- Experience conducting quality inspections in live data center environments with engagement of contractors, engineers, and stakeholders
jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS) designs, plans, delivers, and operates all AWS global infrastructure. We keep the cloud running by supporting AWS data centers and the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that give customers continual access to innovation. We solve complex problems with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain.

You'll join software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other essential roles. You'll collaborate across AWS to deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing capacity at the lowest cost for customers.

In this role, you will:

Work with construction teams to integrate quality assurance into data center network pathway infrastructure construction throughout the project lifecycle. You'll participate in construction design reviews, page turns, and reviews for construction documents, providing Quality Assurance representation to ensure standards are met during construction. You'll influence construction managers, general contractors, Colocation providers and relevant contractors to adopt quality assurance checklists that validate conformance to both Amazon and jurisdictional standards during critical construction milestone checks for pathway infrastructure.

You'll bring the voice of the customer forward by gathering feedback from construction teams and internal stakeholders to refine quality processes. You'll verify that pathway infrastructure construction meets installation requirements by reviewing documentation and data from milestone inspections. You'll document and align on construction standards and standard operating procedures as new technology and construction methods integrate into our infrastructure. You’ll also conduct training with construction managers, and provide guidelines to general contractors, Colocation providers and contractors to ensure consistent execution across all construction activities.

This role matters because construction quality directly impacts data center reliability and customer experience. You'll own the quality process from participating in construction design reviews through training delivery, ensuring our infrastructure meets the standards our customers depend on.

This is an opportunity to leverage and grow your existing program management, quality assurance and engineering experience in an innovative and highly collaborative environment where the results of your work will directly impact AWS and its customers.

Key job responsibilities
- Own Quality Assurance related programs to drive world-class quality in network infrastructure in the new construction of Data Centers to achieve the best operating, lowest cost infrastructure in the world. The Network Infra Quality Engineer - Construction will be obsessed with quality, engineering, and design continuous improvements

- Review cable tray designs against Amazon’s Policies and Industry Standards and define acceptance checklists for each build milestone

- Partner with Construction Management team and Technical Infrastructure Project Manager to ensure network pathway infrastructures are built according to design documents and build project milestones, document early signals for risks and escalate to respective owners for remediations

- Capture requirement variants between approved design and physical installation and collaborate with onsite teams and design teams to close gaps in pathway infrastructure installation methods.

- Define and maintain updates of Quality Assurance documents including inspection checklists and installation requirements from design specifications, Quality Assurance standard operating procedures, Global Design Standards documents, Global Installation Best Practice Guidelines, Product Specifications for Inside Plant cable routing, and Quality Issue trackers.

- Monitor installations and collect lessons learned on network pathway quality

- Define Network Pathway Infrastructure Quality Training Programs, and the creation of training materials, to support Construction teams and as reference guidelines for vendors.

- Define quality and operational metrics related to construction performance, establish measurement frameworks, and analyze data to drive strategic improvements and inform decision-making

- Regional and Local Travel up to 25% to perform site visits and meetings for onsite quality standards review and collect feedback from onsite personnel

A day in the life

The AWS Quality Team is a fast-growing global team that is rethinking the implementation and design for the critical arteries that connect the various pieces of hardware inside AWS’ data centers and edge sites.

As a member of this team, you will be charged with visiting our data centers and ensuring that we are following AWS and Industry standards while also collecting lessons learned so we are continually improving. You will work directly with engineers, managers, and operations teams across the company to develop and improve network pathway and installation standards based on these observations and lessons learned.

jobs.description:
Amazon's vision is to enable customers to find, discover, and buy anything online. We are looking for a smart, results-oriented Associate - Site Merchandising to be a part of growing business. The successful candidate will be responsible for planning and executing best in class on-site, and off-platform merchandising strategies. This role is based in Bangalore.

As an Associate - Site Merchandising, you will ensure that customer experience and expectations are set and met to a high standard. He or she will execute and schedule content on social media, homepage, category landing pages, and other program pages across the site. Also, the candidate will be expected to prepare a report on performance and traffic data to our management teams. The candidate will assist in creation of new brand and product stores based on customer intelligence. This person will be involved in coordinating with the category managers, designers, and other editors in similar roles across the business.

Key job responsibilities
Main responsibilities will include:
· Complete ownership of store merchandising and curation
· Running and analyzing proof of performance reports
· Learning Merchandising tools and widgets
· Assist execution of the plan as per site merchandising calendar. This includes email programs, new product launches, cross-category placements, developing and executing Amazon site-wide events, browse tree design and search results
· Assist in developing merchandising for large store-wide promotions, and work closely with category managers and merchandising team to schedule promotions
· Support the creation of push notifications, category pages, brand stores, marketing campaigns, and develop and manage free search improvement projects
· Synthesize and analyze relevant content/traffic metrics.

A day in the life
The Senior Site Merchandiser must be able to think and act both strategically and tactically. The ideal candidate will demonstrate the following:
· Designing and executing online campaigns which improve customer experience
· Strong communication skills; experience in coordinating teams and communicating to Category Managers
· Strong bias for action and ability to prioritize
· Ability to use hard data and metrics to back up feature and customer segment recommendations
· Working independently on key deliverables
